title: Swings In Time - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: Ashover village playground has changed a lot since I used to play there as a child. All the old climbing frame, see-saw and large slide have gone, replaced with modern safety conscious alternatives. When viewed at a glance changes may be sharp and obvious, but look for a while and you start to notice the similarities. Multiple flash strobes were used to freeze the motion of the swings and allow the image to be taken in a single exposure.
exif: 60 sec Shutter Speed, f/6.3 Aperture, 125 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: A photograph of some swings frozen in time using light painting, set against a backdrop of stars and dramatic clouds.

title: Ashover Light Railway - Nightcaster
description: Located in Fallgate, Ashover, these derelict huts and single length of track are all that remain of this part of the Ashover Light Railway. The railway was closed on 31st March 1950 however a small length remained in place around the Fallgate works and continued to be used until 1968. The heritage of the Ashover Light Railway is now being preserved by the Ashover Light Railway Society, now based in Rowsley. Multiple flash strobes were used to light the huts from the inside and a combination of flash strobes and a flashlight were used to paint the exterior with light.
exif: 180 sec Shutter Speed, f/13.0 Aperture, 160 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: The last remnants of the Ashover Light Railway in Fallgate, Ashover.

title: Sutton Scarsdale Hall - Nightcaster
description: Built between 1724 and 1729 Sutton Scarsdale hall has been roofless since 1919. Featuring an exterior carved from mellow sandstone by Edward Poynton of Nottingham the hall is currently preserved by English Heritage. The image was simply lit with a single flashlight.
exif: 50 sec Shutter Speed, f/5.0 Aperture, 400 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Sutton Scarsdale Hall stands, lit up, in front of a dramatic night sky.

title: Tree of Light - Nightcaster
description: Located around Hardwick Hall ponds are various carved wooden sculptures and signposts. This simple sculpture contains strips of acrylic sheet embedded within that reach from one side to the next. These act to channel light through them. The background was lit with a flashlight and the sculpture was backlit with the same flashlight to light up the acrylic.
exif: 50 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.5 Aperture, 400 ISO, 35mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Backlit acrylic within a wooden sculpture found at Hardwick Hall, Derbyshire.

title: Star Guide - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: Turning Stone Edge sits within Matlock Forest and features stunning views to the East out over Ogston Reservoir. Within the forest lies this tree, which was using it's branches to point at the constellation of the big dipper in the sky above. The tree was lit using a combination of flashlight and strobe and the glow in the forest behind was caused by a campfire that some really friendly local people use regularly.
exif: 30 sec Shutter Speed, f/2.8 Aperture, 1600 ISO, 14mm Focal Length, Samyang 14mm f2.8 Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: A set of twisted branches from a fallen tree point towards the big dipper in the sky above.

title: The Band - Nightcaster
description: A single flashgun was used to create multiple strobes behind the band members as they moved around in place. The use of a shutter delay timer allowed me time to get into position and a long exposure gave enough time to move around in the background and manually strobe the flash.
exif: 20 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.0 Aperture, 640 ISO, 60mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Light painting a local band using multiple flashes in a single long exposure.

title: For Amy - Nightcaster
description: I have done a number of weddings as an official photographer, so when I got married I obviously wanted to take a couple of special pictures in my own style. Unfortunately I only had chance to take the one photo, with only a few minutes to set up and take the picture. Slightly inebriated I took 3 remotely triggered flashguns and placed them along the edge of the treeline angled upwards. With my wife in the scene I positioned a fourth flashgun on the floor behind as a backlight. I took a test exposure to make sure everything was aligned and then ran into the shot. Our official photographer was then tasked with triggering the shutter.
exif: 1/30 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.0 Aperture, 500 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: Using flash strobes to light up a treeline behind a bride and groom.

title: Purple Stone - Ashover Rock - The Fabrick - Nightcaster
description: I spent a lot of my childhood and adolescence at The Fabric, otherwise known as Ashover Rock. It is somewhere that is very special to me, which might explain why I love to light paint it. At 229 meters above sea level, the Fabrick sits high above the village of Ashover and provides views as far as Lincoln Cathedral, over 40 miles away, when conditions are good. Taken towards the end of the blue hour the rock is simply lit with a flashlight from a single angle.
exif: 0.4 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.0 Aperture, 160 ISO, 40mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Ashover Rock, also known as The Fabrick, lit up by a flashlight at the end of the blue hour.

title: Syzygy Over Barn - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: A syzygy is an astronomical term for a conjunction or opposition. On the night this photo was taken Venus, Mars and the Moon were all in a straight line in the sky. Venus can be seen as the brightest object after the moon just above the left of the barn. Mars is the red dot that can be found by drawing a line directly from Venus through the Moon. The barn was lit using a remote flash inside the barn hidden behind one of the support poles, and another off to the left of the frame. A flashlight with a yellow gel was used to light the exterior of the barn.
exif: 16 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.0 Aperture, 1600 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: Light painting a derelict barn as a syzygy of the Moon, Venus and Mars takes place in the sky above.

title: Sola Arbor - Derbyshire - Nightcaster
description: Sola Arbor translates from Latin as "Lonely Tree". This beautiful tree sits alone on the top of a gentle hill, making it really stand out as the landscape falls away behind it. The tree was lit using two flashguns off to the right of the tree just over the brow of the hill to hide them from the camera. One was set to a tight beam with a yellow filter and aimed towards the top of the tree. The other was set to a wide spread with an orange filter and aimed towards the base/middle of the tree. A flashlight was used off to the right of the frame to paint in the foreground.
exif: 15 sec Shutter Speed, f/4.0 Aperture, 1600 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: A lone tree stands on a hill, lit by flashguns and a flashlight, under a starry night sky.

title: Ravensnest Copse - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: A copse is a small group of trees, and this particular one can be found near Ravensnest Tor, Ashover. The trees were lit using a single flashlight aimed directly upwards under the trees as I walked along the side of the wall while the shutter was open. A remote timer release really helped as I could get into position and trigger the shutter when ready.
exif: 25 sec Shutter Speed, f/2.8 Aperture, 800 ISO, 14mm Focal Length, Samyang 14mm f2.8 Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: Light painting a copse of trees against a night sky full of stars.

title: Helping Hand - Fabrick Rock - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: Exploring Ashover Rock (otherwise known as the Fabrick) is fascinating for people of all ages. There's much more to discover than might be immediately visible. Luckily I was there to take this picture as a Grandfather helped his Grandson down from the top of the rock after a thorough investigation.
exif: 1/50 sec Shutter Speed, f/13.0 Aperture, 160 ISO, 47mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: A Grandfather helps his Grandson down from the top of Ashover Rock during a beautiful sunset.

title: Misty Fells - Hallin Fell - Lake District - Nightcaster
description: Looking towards Hallin Fell in the Lake District the misty weather sits in the valleys between the peaks. The large cairn at the peak of Hallin Fell can be seen beneath the dramatic clouds.
exif: 1/40 sec Shutter Speed, f/8.0 Aperture, 100 ISO, 105mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: The misty weather sits in the valleys between the peaks of the Lake District.

title: Into The Mist - Errwood Reservoir - Goyt Valley - Nightcaster
description: I have only ever visted the Goyt Valley once that I can rembember, as we popped by on the journey to a weekend away. Visibility was extremely low but I really liked the way the dam at Errwood Reservoir disappeared into the fog.
exif: 1/13 sec Shutter Speed, f/16.0 Aperture, 160 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: The dam wall of Errwood Reservoir in the Goyt Valley disappears into a thick fog.

title: Hallin Fell - Ullswater - Cumbria - Nightcaster
description: The large cairn at the peak of Hallin Fell near Ullswater in Cumbria is bathed in the last sunlight of the day.
exif: 1/20 sec Shutter Speed, f/9.0 Aperture, 100 ISO, 200mm Focal Length, EF70-200mm f/4L Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: The peak of Hallin Fell in the Lake District, bathed in the last sunlight of the day.

title: Ullswater Vista - Lake District - Nightcaster
description: This panoramic of Ullswater in the Lake District was taken from the top of the hill at Aria Force. 3 Images were stitched together to create the panoramic.
exif: 1/125 sec Shutter Speed, f/10.0 Aperture, 100 ISO, 28mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 6D Camera.
meta_description: A panoramic photograph of Ullswater in the Lake District, taken from the top of the hill at Aira Force.

title: Standing Before the Stand - Crich Stand - Nightcaster
description: The memorial tower (also known as Crich Stand) was completed in 1923 and stands to the memory of the soldiers from the Sherwood Foresters Regiment who died in World War I and World War II. The stand is also dedicated to others who have died in service during other conflicts. A simple image showing my friend as he captures the stand from a different angle.
exif: 1/40 sec Shutter Speed, f/5.0 Aperture, 400 ISO, 24mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Crich Stand (the Sherwood Foresters Memorial) stands at the top of Crich Hill in Derbyshire.

title: Leaves - Nightcaster
description: Sitting atop countless submerged leaves lies a single maple leaf, still floating gently on the waters surface. A small bead of water has been trapped on top of the leaf.
exif: 1/320 sec Shutter Speed, f/29.0 Aperture, 640 ISO, 109mm Focal Length, EF70-200mm f/4L Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: A maple leaf floats on the surface of some water on top of some submerged leaves.

title: Twin Trees - Ashover - Nightcaster
description: The two trees almost look like they have been mirrored, each growing apart at a similar angle, to a similar height. The last light of a December day makes the haze glow as though it's still sunset, even though the sun had set over 15 minutes before.
exif: 2 sec Shutter Speed, f/9.0 Aperture, 125 ISO, 105mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: Two trees stand in a misty field as the sun sets over the Derbyshire countryside.

title: Perched on the Edge - Norfolk - Nightcaster
description: When the sun is out during that period most people refer to as "daytime" I often find my landscape images become more focused on patterns and textures. I love to get what I call "wallpaper" shots, as they are mostly very flat and make ideal desktop wallpapers for the computer. This shot was taken from the car park after a lovely walk down the beach in Norfolk.
exif: 1/400 sec Shutter Speed, f/14.0 Aperture, 400 ISO, 105mm Focal Length, EF24-105mm f/4L IS Lens, Canon EOS 5D Camera.
meta_description: A seagull perched on the edge of a pebble beach in Norfolk.

title: Yfirgefnar
description:
Yfirgefnar means "abandoned" in Icelandic.
This image was taken during my all to brief mid-winter weekend in Iceland.

title: Horizon on Fire
description:
Looking into the sunset as the sun falls behind the far treeline. It almost looks as if the horizon is ablaze.
Taken in 2005.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: The setting sun makes the horizon appear to be burning

title: Sheepish Silhouette
description: A single sheep stands, silhouetted against the sunset at Curbar Gap.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A single sheep stands, silhouetted against the sunset at Curbar Gap.

title: Twisted Trees
description:
By using a slightly extended shutter speed and rotating the camera around the lens axis while depressing the shutter button you can create some really interesting images.
This image was taken in Hardwick wood near Wingerworth.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A simple image of trees enhanced by moving the camera to create circles

title: Mixed Media
description: A simple tightly framed photograph of an old barn. The use of black and white takes the focus away from the subject and serves to really highlight the different textures within the image.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A simple black and white shot of a barn showing stone wood and hay textures

title: Fishermans Forgotten Friend
description:
Far from the shoreline on a beach in Norfolk lies this derelict boat.
Processed to black and white to add focus to the large range of textures within the image.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A black and white shot of a derelict boat near a beach in Norfolk

title: Star Gazer
description: The StarDisc in Wirksworth was unveiled on September 10th 2011 and offers a 21st century interpretation of a stone circle. Created from black granite the disk is surrounded by 12 illuminated benches and features a star chart carved into the main disk. It provides an excellent vantage point for star gazing and a beautiful view of the surrounding landscape. No additional lighting was added to the exposure, all LEDs and light sources are part of the StarDisc itself.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A photo showing someone gazing at the stars whilst at the StarDisk in Wirksworth, Derbyshire

title: Summer Solstice
description: The 2009 summer solstice landed on a Saturday and as a result saw a record number of over 36,500 people attending Stonehenge. This long exposure nightscape has technically been light painted, with lasers shone on the stones and coloured leds dancing throughout the frame. As none of this was intentional or added by myself I consider this a nightscape.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: Long exposure of the summer solstice at Stonehenge in 2009

title: Lunar Eclipse - September 2015
description: The rare blood moon supermoon total lunar eclipse from September 2015. Taken from the edge of Hardwick wood near Wingerworth.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: The rare blood moon supermoon total lunar eclipse from September 2015

title: Belper Mill Bridge
description: Belper Mill is an industrial revolution era cotton mill which is now a Museum and offices. Also known as Strutt's North Mill in Belper, it is one of the Derwent Valley Mills and was given UNESCO World Heritage Status in 2001.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: The road passes under a bridge with offices above.

title: Heage Windmill
description: Built in 1797 and restored in 2002, Heage Windmill is a Grade II listed building and is the only working six-sailed stone tower windmill in England, set in the Derbyshire countryside.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: The windmill at Heage stands in front of a graduated night sky

title: Circles
description: The Mollusc sculpture by James Woodford is located outside the main library in Chesterfield. It is cast from bronze and was unveiled in 1964.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A close up of the Mollusc sculpture in Chesterfield, Derbyshire

title: Eosophobia
description: Eosophobia is a rare morbid fear of dawn or daylight. Taken in 2004 this is one of my first nightscapes.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A silhouette stands in front of a burning sky

title: Impending Doom
description: Taken on the moors near sheffield, an impromptu shot shows a car approaching from over a small hill. This photograph is from 2004 and was one of my first nightscapes. I still like the mood that this photograph has and the composition enhances the dark and foreboading feeling.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A car crests a hill on the moors near Sheffield

title: Lakeside
description: Taken in 2005 as I had begun to refine my style, this image was a lesson in composition. Just in front of the frame was a small, dark, muddy island and to either side of the trees are homes, signs, lamposts etc.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: Some trees are reflected in a pond to create a circle

title: Stainsby 2006
description: The Stainsby Festival is a small, well-established and intimate festival in Derbyshire. It is a family-friendly event with a wide variety of music, workshops, and entertainment. This photograph captures a group of friends sitting around outside a tent as the sun begins to set.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A group of friends sit around outside a tent at Stainsby 2006

title: Star Disk
description: The StarDisc in Wirksworth was unveiled on September 10th 2011 and offers a 21st century interpretation of a stone circle. Created from black granite the disk is surrounded by 12 illuminated benches and features a star chart carved into the main disk. It provides an excellent vantage point for star gazing and a beautiful view of the surrounding landscape. No additional lighting was added to the exposure, all LEDs and light sources are part of the StarDisc itself.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A photo showing the view whilst at the StarDisk in Wirksworth, Derbyshire

title: Trapped
description: Sutton Scarsdale Hall was a stately home in Derbyshire. It was built in 1724 but was sadly gutted in 1919 and has been a ruin ever since. It is now managed by English Heritage. This image was taken from within the ruin, looking out through a window at a tree.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A tree viewed from within Sutton Scarsdale Hall

title: Alport Stone
description: The Alport Stone can be found just below the summit of Alport Height, near Wirksworth in Derbyshire. The stone itself is over 6.1m (20 ft) high and has a number of recognised climbing routes up it. I lit this image by placing a couple of remote flashes at different angles to the stone, one off to the right of the frame, about 15 meters away facing the stone to light the scene, and another to the left of the frame and further away than the stone. This was pointed backwards to face the camera and set to a very low power in order to provide some fill light to the darkest shadows cast by the main light.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A beam of light reaches to the stars in this long exposure light painted photograph of the Alport Stone in Derbyshire

title: Lumen Flumen Lectum
description: "The Light River Bed" As the clouds streak overhead I am reminded that we live on the bed of an ocean of air, with currents that flow like rivers through the sky. This long exposure image was made using the full moon to light the scene, with a flash off to the right of the camera to gently light the tree.

meta_tags.name: description
meta_tags.content: A lone tree is lit below a starry sky as the clouds streak overhead

title: Lignum Annulos
description: "Tree Rings" Quite often the best word to describe light writing like this is simply "fun". It provides a great excuse to act like a child all over again in the name of creating some art. This image was created by lighting the tree with a couple of flashes set off to the left of the frame whilst I danced around the tree with a sparkler.

title: Magneticam Ligno
description: "Magnetic Tree" This was created on my friends driveway (lucky bugger) using a sparkler attached to the end of a light stand. We had decided to try and experiment with sparklers, fire and wire wool, to see what we could achieve and wanted to do so in a safe and controled location. My friend walked around the tree while moving the light stand in an arc to create the lines you see. The rest of the scene is lit using ambient lighting from my friends house and the glow from the sparkler.

title: Clapper Bridge
description: It's always great to try and meet up with fellow photographers and this image was created with one such fellow light painter. We had decided to visit Dartmoor for some night photography, and Clapper Bridge was one of the locations we had in mind. We arrived at the bridge and set up our gear, then spent the next few hours light painting the bridge and the surrounding area. We took about 3 practice exposures, figuring out how much light to paint and where, before we got the final image. At some point during this time we heard a splash. Fearing a repeat of my previous visit we franticly searched and checked our equipment to make sure we had everything and nothing had fallen in. Dismissing it as a stone falling from the riverbank or some animal, we carried on as usual. It was about 10 minutes later when my friend cursed slightly and dove his hand into the river, only to retrieve my remote flash trigger unit, pooring with water. I immediately turned it off (the LED power indicators were still on!) and took the batteries out, before pouring out what seemed like half the river from inside the trigger and drying the unit as best as possible. We decided to move on, but not call it a night, so we worked our way back down the footpath towards the car, taking more photos as we went, but manually triggering flashes when we used them! I returned home and immediately dissasembled the flash trigger, dried it's innards as best as I could with some kitchen roll, and placed the pieces in a small metal tin which I then balanced on the radiator, ready for when it would heat up in the morning. I left it to dry for 12 hours before checking for any obvious signs of remaining moisture or water damage, but couldn't see anything. I re-assembled the trigger, inserted some freshly charged batteries and it turned on just fine and still worked as it should. In fact it's still working just fine to this day, over 9 months after it's bath. I will be reviewing the trigger and remote flashes at some point in the near future, so please come back soon for that.

title: Make Your Own Path
description: I love these paving slabs and the footpath they are part of. It's such a beautiful part of Ashover. By attaching my torch to a light stand I was able to follow the cracks and lines between the pavers without getting too much of myself in the image. I pointed the torch towards the floor rather than at the camera and tried to move as quickly and fluidly as possible while following the lines as close as possible. Once I was out of the frame a couple of flash strobes were used to light the main scene.

title: Inde Alveus
description: "Reservoir Side" Clouds prevented me from seeing the peak of the Geminids meteor shower, but I did make it out two nights later. Sadly no meteors while I was out, and heavy frost on my camera and flashes meant I had to leave after just over an hour, but I did manage to get a couple of images, so it was certainly worth it. The tree was lit with a flash from the left at 90° to the tree to light within the branches & another flash about 45° to the left to light the whole scene. No other lighting was used.

title: Gateway to the Stars
description: Milky Way season had nearly passed and I had missed every opportunity either due to bad weather or sheer lazyness, so when I noticed the forecast was clear during the last new moon of the season I charged my batteries, filled my flask with tea and headed out for a night under the stars...

title: Din Lights in the Sky
description: The stars twinkled gently overhead and the the Milky Way arched silently, calmly across the sky. About 500 meters away behind the treeline a shed full of cows had decided that the peace and quiet could last no longer...

title: West Bank
description: Shortlisted in the Matlock Bath Pro Loco Photography Competition 2021

title: Winding
description: Shortlisted in the Matlock Bath Pro Loco Photography Competition 2021

title: Worship
description: Shortlisted in the Matlock Bath Pro Loco Photography Competition 2021

title: Waypoint
description: The Holy Trinity Church in Matlock Bath rises from the canopy.

title: Shields Holding Fast
description: This is as seen from High Tor looking South West(!) towards Matlock Bath. It was so bright and structure was clearly visible to the naked eye. Could even begin to make out some colours. Foreground lit by torch from the left of frame.

title: Magical Matlock Bath
description: What could be better than a lovely big mug of tea while sitting and watching the aurora dance in the sky?

title: Celestial Canvas
description: The aurora dance in the sky over artists corner in the lower left and Matlock in the distance.

title: Domus sub Aurora
description: Domus sub Aurora (House under the Aurora). I love this house. It's so idillically perfect in every way. Both it and the grounds are meticulously maintained. The aurora shines in the sky, with the house lit by a very subtle, low-power fill light from a torch to the left of the frame. I only got one image because I didn't want to disturb the people sleeping in the house.

title: Picta Nox
description: Picta Nox (Painted Night). The aurora dance in the sky over Matlock as seen from High Tor.
